title: Dangers On A Train

jokes.text: In your face, people who said it wouldn't last a year!
jokes.setup: Marge celebrating their first anniversary
jokes.punchline: Aggressively defending their marriage to imaginary critics
jokes.explanation: Classic Marge passive-aggression disguised as celebration. Good character-specific humor with nice economy.

jokes.text: I stand by my wedding toast.
jokes.setup: Marge's defensive statement about their marriage lasting
jokes.punchline: Homer doubling down on his apparently pessimistic wedding toast
jokes.explanation: Perfectly economical Homer response that reveals his wedding toast was apparently doom-and-gloom. Great character consistency and timing.

jokes.text: Accidental motherhood is the greatest thing that can happen to a woman.
jokes.setup: Marge trying to be positive about their relationship
jokes.punchline: Describing motherhood as 'accidental' while calling it the greatest thing
jokes.explanation: Marge's attempt at positivity undermined by her word choice. Decent character moment but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Oops, better clean that off.
jokes.setup: Marge just said Bart has outgrown his misbehavior
jokes.punchline: Immediate cut to Bart doing something that needs cleaning up
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing - instant contradiction of Marge's claim. Classic Simpsons visual storytelling.

jokes.text: Wish I could get a little of that attention.
jokes.setup: Someone being polite to Marge
jokes.punchline: Homer jealous of basic courtesy
jokes.explanation: Homer's neediness showing through in a relatable way. Good character work but unremarkable.

jokes.text: Don't mean to be a pesto, but I'll be happy to watch your scampi shrimp.
jokes.setup: Flanders offering to babysit
jokes.punchline: Using Italian food terms instead of 'pest' and 'scamp'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Flanders-ism. Creative wordplay that feels completely natural for his character. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Daddy. He's not your daddy. Nice daddy. Moustache daddy. Good daddy no yell. Fat daddy smell like beer.
jokes.setup: Maggie showing preference for Ned
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific and damning comparisons to Homer
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from baby talk to brutal honesty, each detail more damning

jokes.text: Oh, Homer, you're the anchor store of my heart.
jokes.setup: Romantic moment at the mall
jokes.punchline: Marge using shopping mall terminology for romance
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific metaphor that perfectly captures mall romance setting

jokes.text: Just call me Borders Books, 'cause I'll always be here.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to be romantic about permanence
jokes.punchline: Comparing himself to a famously failed bookstore chain
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cultural reference joke. Homer's romantic gesture becomes accidentally prophetic about failure. Perfect economy and very quotable.

jokes.text: His first word.
jokes.setup: Nelson's signature 'Haw haw!' laugh
jokes.punchline: Someone treating it as a baby's first word
jokes.explanation: Clever use of Nelson's catchphrase in new context. Good economy and character integration.

jokes.text: Sometimes you have to say to hell with rules.
jokes.setup: Being told they have to get off and back on the train
jokes.punchline: Homer treating this minor inconvenience as civil disobedience
jokes.explanation: Homer's tendency to overdramatize minor situations. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Wait, you named me after a train?
jokes.setup: Homer explaining they're recreating their first anniversary on a train
jokes.punchline: Lisa realizing the origin of her name
jokes.explanation: Clever reveal that recontextualizes Lisa's name. Good setup/payoff structure with believable character reaction.

jokes.text: Yeah, just like we did with Bart.
jokes.setup: Lisa learning she was named after a train
jokes.punchline: Bart was also apparently named after a train
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that suggests both kids were named randomly. Excellent economy in the delivery.

jokes.text: Medical marijuana dispensary interaction
jokes.setup: Mall replacing train with medical marijuana stand
jokes.punchline: Worker diagnosing everyone with marijuana-treatable conditions
jokes.explanation: Topical humor about medical marijuana dispensaries. Competent but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: I've only had three great ideas-- marrying your mother, using a hot dog as a straw and this romantic train ride anniversary.
jokes.setup: Homer listing his life's great ideas
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous hot dog straw idea sandwiched between legitimate accomplishments
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - mixing profound and absurd with complete sincerity. Great structure and character integration.

jokes.text: Hey, babies-- where you going in your baby train? Babytown?
jokes.setup: Nelson mocking the family on their kiddie train
jokes.punchline: His elaborate baby-themed insult
jokes.explanation: Classic Nelson bullying with his characteristic creativity. Good character voice.

jokes.text: Hey, lardo, where you going in your lardo car? Lardville? There's no such place!
jokes.setup: Kids trying to comeback at Nelson using his own format
jokes.punchline: They realize mid-insult that their fictional place doesn't work
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about the logic of insults. Kids' attempt at Nelson's formula falls apart in real-time.

jokes.text: That's Mozart, dummy.
jokes.setup: Someone mentioning 'Baby Beethovens'
jokes.punchline: Lisa correcting the classical music reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa moment - correcting cultural references. Good character integration but predictable.

jokes.text: Yeah, what size cuff links do you get an elephant?
jokes.setup: Selma saying anniversary gifts must be tough
jokes.punchline: Patty's cruel comparison of Homer to an elephant
jokes.explanation: Classic Patty cruelty toward Homer. Good character consistency and decent setup/payoff.

jokes.text: Well, in the first place, the elephant's cuff links would be the same size. It's the cuffs that would be bigger.
jokes.setup: Patty's elephant comparison
jokes.punchline: Selma seriously analyzing the logistics of elephant formal wear
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deadpan response that takes the absurd premise seriously. Great unexpected direction for the joke.

jokes.text: Sassy Madison is a dating site for married people who are tired of the same old dessert.
jokes.setup: Marge thinking she's ordering snack cakes
jokes.punchline: The double entendre of 'dessert' in a cheating website's tagline
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that drives the plot. Good double meaning with 'dessert' working on multiple levels.

jokes.text: It's not cheating if you don't know the person well.
jokes.setup: Cheating website's advertisement
jokes.punchline: Completely backwards logic about what makes cheating acceptable
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about infidelity with twisted logic. Good satirical edge and very quotable.

jokes.text: And this town's got losers like Mexico has headless corpses.
jokes.setup: Talking about losers hitting on Marge online
jokes.punchline: Extremely dark comparison to drug cartel violence
jokes.explanation: Shockingly dark humor from Selma. Good economy but the darkness might not land for all audiences.

jokes.text: Maybe I should get married so I can date these guys.
jokes.setup: Looking at all the men on the cheating site
jokes.punchline: Backwards logic of getting married to access married dating
jokes.explanation: Good Selma character moment with twisted dating logic. Solid structure and character integration.

jokes.text: Are you watching the commercial again?
jokes.setup: Homer apparently rewatching the Sassy Madison ad
jokes.punchline: Marge catching him and his weak excuse
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment of Homer being caught. Decent character dynamics but not exceptional.

jokes.text: I'm a fan of animation.
jokes.setup: Being caught watching a cheating website commercial
jokes.punchline: Meta joke about The Simpsons being animated
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-humor that works on multiple levels. Homer's excuse accidentally references the show's medium.

jokes.text: 'Laser.' That's a funny word. It's just 'loser' with an 'A.'
jokes.setup: Homer trying to focus 'like a laser'
jokes.punchline: His observation about laser/loser similarity
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay observation that feels very Homer. Good character-specific humor with nice economy.

jokes.text: And speaking of losers, I lost my train of thought.
jokes.setup: Just made the laser/loser connection
jokes.punchline: Connecting 'losers' to losing his train of thought
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up wordplay that maintains the momentum. Decent structure and character consistency.

jokes.text: He remembers the exact number of pork chops in the freezer. Currently-- zero.
jokes.setup: Marge frustrated that Homer forgets important things
jokes.punchline: But he knows exactly how many pork chops there are (none)
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer characterization - remembering food-related details while forgetting everything else. Good setup/payoff.

jokes.text: Oh, good, the strippers are here. Oh, yeah. Oh, yeah! That is all coming off.
jokes.setup: Homer appearing to watch strippers
jokes.punchline: He's actually excited about wallpaper removal for the train renovation
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection joke. Sets up one expectation then reveals innocent alternative. Good structure and earned surprise.

jokes.text: Hey, those Yelp reviews don't write themselves.
jokes.setup: Marge asking why he was on computer until 4am
jokes.punchline: Homer's dedication to writing negative reviews
jokes.explanation: Good modern observational humor about online review culture. Fits Homer's petty nature well.

jokes.text: Did you know a well-placed one star can destroy a mom-and-pop hardware in nothing flat?
jokes.setup: Homer talking about Yelp reviews
jokes.punchline: His pride in destroying small businesses with bad reviews
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about review culture's power. Good character moment showing Homer's casual cruelty.

jokes.text: How stupid are you? Hey, thinking is your department, jerk. That's what I pay you in beer for.
jokes.setup: Homer arguing with his own brain
jokes.punchline: Treating his brain as a separate employee he pays in beer
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer internal monologue humor. Perfect character integration with absurd brain-as-employee concept.

jokes.text: 'A' is for 'apple,' right?
jokes.setup: Ben trying to help with a stain using store products
jokes.punchline: His apparent illiteracy or extreme nervousness
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Ben's nervousness. Decent character work but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Nothing turns leftovers into swans like aluminum.
jokes.setup: Marge talking about their aluminum anniversary
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical claim about aluminum's transformative properties
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd Marge moment. Good character voice with poetic absurdity.

jokes.text: What the heck am I saying?
jokes.setup: Marge just said something nonsensical about aluminum
jokes.punchline: Self-aware moment about her own absurd statement
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment where character acknowledges their own absurdity. Nice callback structure.

jokes.text: Homer's probably at that bar right now, getting hammered.
jokes.setup: Marge assuming Homer is drinking
jokes.punchline: Cut to Homer literally hammering train parts
jokes.explanation: Good visual pun with literal vs figurative 'hammered'

jokes.text: He says shows set during World War I are too sad. Particularly how everyone's always climbing stairs.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Homer doesn't watch period dramas
jokes.punchline: Homer's bizarrely specific complaint about stair-climbing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic - finding the most random detail to focus on. Perfect character voice and very quotable.

jokes.text: Save that sugar for your coffee, bub.
jokes.setup: Ben getting flirtatious with Marge
jokes.punchline: Moe's crude interruption with sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe character moment with crude humor. Good character integration but relies on lowbrow appeal.

jokes.text: Because it's in the public domain.
jokes.setup: Kids asking why he's singing 'I've Been Working on the Railroad'
jokes.punchline: Meta joke about The Simpsons using royalty-free music
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-humor about TV production realities. Great economy and industry insider knowledge.

jokes.text: Why the hell are you calling me at home, you moron?! / Lovejoy went to get a hamburger, and the whole thing fell apart!
jokes.setup: Homer telling Marge that Moe needs to hear a friendly voice
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately screaming at Moe / Moe's dramatic explanation
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke with perfect character voices. Homer's hypocrisy and Moe's desperation work well together.

jokes.text: Mom, Bart's twisting facts in an Orwellian fashion.
jokes.setup: Kids tattling on each other
jokes.punchline: Lisa's overly intellectual complaint about Bart
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character moment - using literary references for simple sibling complaints. Great character integration.

jokes.text: Orwell, obscure? The author of Animal Farm? / Grampa says he was a Commie.
jokes.setup: Marge calling Orwell an obscure reference
jokes.punchline: Lisa's indignation followed by Bart's politically ignorant addition
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast between Lisa's intellectualism and Bart's casual repetition of Grampa's nonsense.

jokes.text: Come on, Taser that flamingo!
jokes.setup: Watching a reality show about repossessing zoo animals
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastic encouragement of animal cruelty
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd reality TV concept. Dark humor with great economy and quotable line.

jokes.text: Once again, India is denuded of her treasure by the imperialist devils.
jokes.setup: Period drama character giving away an Indian ring
jokes.punchline: Another character's political commentary on colonialism
jokes.explanation: Decent satire of period drama tropes and historical awareness. Good but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Why do we watch this show? / Because I like the costumes!
jokes.setup: Character questioning their show's politics
jokes.punchline: Shallow reason for watching continues despite problematic content
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on why people watch problematic entertainment. Decent but unremarkable.

jokes.text: Three kids with no money, but plenty of love!
jokes.setup: Kids giving anniversary present
jokes.punchline: Cheerfully admitting they have no money
jokes.explanation: Sweet moment with underlying economic reality humor

jokes.text: Yeah. I sell 'em on the Internet for like-minded people.
jokes.setup: Homer commenting on Moe's shrub costume
jokes.punchline: Moe's implication that he sells camouflage costumes to perverts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe character moment with creepy business venture. Good character integration.

jokes.text: When does it get better? When he's 800 pounds and has to be cut out of the house to go to a movie?
jokes.setup: Ramona complaining about her marriage
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific and dark prediction of husband's future
jokes.explanation: Dark and specific imagery that escalates marital complaints to absurd level

jokes.text: I lost my cleaning stick in my belly fat. But I found a kitten.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy complaining about his weight
jokes.punchline: Unexpectedly wholesome discovery in his fat folds
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor with perfect misdirection. Grotesque setup leads to surprisingly sweet payoff.

jokes.text: Whoa, you really went all in with this guy, didn't you?
jokes.setup: Marge mentioning three kids
jokes.punchline: Ben's casual assessment of Marge's commitment level
jokes.explanation: Good casual observation that treats having kids like making a big bet

jokes.text: Happy moment derailed by the Bipolar Express.
jokes.setup: Their romantic train moment interrupted by drama
jokes.punchline: Homer's train pun about emotional instability
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay combining train terminology with mental health. Perfect economy and very quotable.

jokes.text: My marathon time isn't 4:26; it's never,
jokes.setup: Homer confessing his secrets to show honesty
jokes.punchline: Admitting he's never actually run a marathon
jokes.explanation: Good Homer character moment with relatable self-deprecating humor. Nice economy in the punchline.

jokes.text: and I once pushed a kid off a swing and he broke his wrist or something.
jokes.setup: Homer's minor confessions
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of actually harmful act
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation from harmless to actually harmful with characteristic Homer uncertainty

jokes.text: I often go online to see how Lindsay Wagner's looking now-- fabulous--
jokes.setup: Homer listing his secrets
jokes.punchline: His oddly specific celebrity obsession with The Bionic Woman
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor with random celebrity reference. Feels authentically Homer.

jokes.text: Three awesome kids and a life of regret
jokes.setup: Upbeat song about finding love
jokes.punchline: Darkly honest assessment of family life
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor that subverts the upbeat musical number format. Nice economy and honesty.

jokes.text: Down by the old, Not the new, but the old... Mill stream, Not the river, but the stream...
jokes.setup: Grampa starting to sing a song
jokes.punchline: His obsessive need to clarify every word choice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Grampa character moment with his pedantic interruptions. Great character integration.

tmdb_overview: When Marge tries to buy Homer snack cakes for their anniversary, she winds up meeting a married man who shares a common love of British Television. Ignorant of all, Homer attempts to secretly construct a romantic anniversary train in the Simpsons back yard.
